Another great day for semiconductor, photonics, space and energy sectors today.  Overall, AI infrastructure continues to be the strongest theme in this market right now.  
Not everything is working — this is a selective market — but quality names tied to compute, memory, optical networking, power, cooling and space infrastructure are seeing strong money rotation and momentum.  The market keeps rewarding the right sectors and patience.

text:
TLDR: $MTSI – the analog name inside every 800G/1.6T optical module Rubin needs.

Silicon Photonics & CPO (#2 this week) (co-packaged optics, lasers built into the chip) (#2 this week) is the bottleneck.

The volume player in the basket: $MTSI.

Drivers, TIAs, lasers.

They sit where the modules can't ship without them.

🧵

text:
the scale up shift to CPO/NPO/LPO seems more quickly than we expected.

the truth is MAC layer is ready, both NVLINK( and UALink(ETH) are ready for copper/optical PHY in protocol level: they are just waiting for the photonics industry to catch up.

CPO is fully verified on both NVDA switch and AVGO switch with TSMC.

Chinese CSP/GPU:
1. ALI: full optical LPO.
2. Huawei: full optical LPO.

US:
1. Google: full optical LPO + OCS
2. Nvidia: NVLINK mixed copper / optical ,  NVL72 scale-up(copper) ,  8 x NVL72 intra connection(CPO) = NVL576,  scale-out: optical.
full CPO in 2028 on Feyman(NVL1152 full CPO).

the investment to $LITE and $COHR  and $GLW shows NVIDIA is trying intensify the relation and production on optical, and shift to optical. just like one year ago Jensen visited Korea to secure the HBM.

the optical usage is 30X increase than traditional server. and the industry is not ready... as NVIDIA's development is much more fast than the photonics industry.

text:
From Physics classrooms in Nigeria 🇳🇬 to the semiconductor industry in the USA 🇺🇸… still feels surreal sometimes.

I’ll be speaking at the UNESCO International Day of Light 2026 organized by Photonics Nigeria on:

🔬 The Role of Precision Optics in Semiconductor Manufacturing

If you’re curious about semiconductors, photonics, optics, engineering careers, or the future of technology, this session is for you.

📅 May 16, 2026
⏰ 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM WAT
🌐 Online Event

Make sure to help share/retweet so it reach more STEM students! 

See you there! 🚀✨ #Photonics #Semiconductors #Optics #Engineering #Nigeria #ASML #STEM

I’m kickstarting my talk with Photonics Nigeria 🇳🇬 Get in touch with them on LinkedIn and register. 
I can’t wait to speak on this topic and inspire young minds 🚀 https://t.co/g9ZPYPt4wZ

text:
PTs for $M7U / Nynomic:

€27 = current Montega target €30–35 = basic turnaround/SOTP rerate
€40–50 = photonics/semi narrative gets priced
€60+ = strong bull case if LayTec + tec5 + Avantes get discovered

Risk: execution + margins. At ~1.3x sales, asymmetry is interesting.

text:
Some new entries / bids for new entries. 

I firmly believe Robotics will be the H2 bubble of 2026. Think space H2 2025 and photonics H1 2026. 

Aside from the DCA strategy on $BOT. We like $OUST $ABSI and $AMBA from below levels if we get them. 

There are others like $VPG, but missed that boat for my risk tolerance.

Other secondaries will benefit well in this wake on the material side like $USAR (still great entry) and $MP.

text:
What makes $M7U / Nynomic interesting is the hidden SOTP angle.

Most people only look at LayTec, but Nynomic also owns tec5, Avantes, Sensortherm, m-u-t, art photonics, LemnaTec and more.

It’s a basket of photonics/sensorics hidden champions trading like a boring turnaround.

$M7U / Nynomic bull case:

At ~€18–19, Nynomic is a ~€120–130M market cap photonics group guiding for €100–105M FY26 revenue.

That’s only ~1.2–1.3x sales.

For a European photonics/SOTP turnaround with semi, pharma, sensorics, spectroscopy and LayTec exposure, that is cheap.

text:
$POET ✨ 
just announced a major new step in its AI photonics expansion.

POET Technologies secured an initial $50M purchase commitment from Lumilens for next-generation optical networking solutions.

Even more notable:

The partnership could reportedly expand to over $500M in cumulative purchases over the next 5 years.

Key focus areas:
⚡ AI optical networking
⚡ 800G / 1.6T connectivity
⚡ Wafer-level photonic integration
⚡ Hyperscale AI data centers

This is exactly where the AI infrastructure market appears to be heading:
higher bandwidth + lower power + optical scaling.

text:
What the market is mispricing on $CRDO

Did you know $CRDO is the only company that sells the entire interconnect stack - chip, cable, transceiver, photonics, software?

That’s the moat.💥

Bears point to gross margin compression after the $750M DustPhotonics acquisition. They’re missing the trade.

What they bought:
• Silicon Photonics PIC tech in-house
• $500M+ optical revenue path by FY27
• $6B SiPho TAM by 2030
• EPS-accretive in FY27
• Vertical integration into the optical engine

Peer margins (latest reported):
$AAOI: 31%
$LITE: 39%
$CIEN: 43%
$CRDO: 68.6%

$CRDO margin is 25+ points higher than every pure-play optical competitor. 

They’re not commoditizing - they’re consolidating.

The 5 stack:
🔌 Copper AECs - no real competitor at 1.6T
⚡ DSPs + proprietary SerDes IP
💡 ZeroFlap optical transceivers
🔬 Silicon Photonics - now in-house
🧠 PILOT predictive telemetry

*No one else owns all five layers*

Meanwhile their 1.6T ZeroFlap AECs are fully cabling $NVDA flagship Kyber Ultra NVL576 - the most important AI rack of the next 24 months.

Market sees: “expensive stock, margin compression.”

Market is missing: vertical integration into a $700B AI capex cycle, with the only company that owns the entire physical layer.

The market is mispricing the moat. 🚀

Long $CRDO

text:
The 4-Layer Photonics Stack Powering Tomorrow’s AI Clusters  Gemini Trading just released this clean, layered infographic that maps exactly how photonics is replacing copper in AI infrastructure — from silicon to global cloud.  Layer 1 – Chips
Electrical side: DSP + Driver + TIA.
Photonic side: Laser + Modulator + Photodetector. The foundation.  Layer 2 – Photonic Engine & Module
These chips combine into a full Photonic Engine, then packaged as an Optical Module — turning electrical signals into light.  Layer 3 – Packaging Options
• Traditional Pluggable modules
• Near-Packaged Optics (NPO)
• Game-changing Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) — placing optics right beside the switch ASIC for minimal latency and power loss.  Layer 4 – Distances & Network
Light travels across every scale:  Within rack  
Rack-to-rack  
Row/floor  
Data center to data center

Underpinning everything: advanced Material Platforms — InP, Silicon Photonics PIC, and TFLN modulators — each optimized for speed, power, and cost.  Timeline? Expect major deployment acceleration in +6 months and full maturity by +1 year.  This isn’t incremental — it’s the architectural shift hyperscalers need to keep training trillion-parameter models without melting data centers. CPO + silicon photonics = dramatically lower power, higher bandwidth, and sustainable AI scaling.  At Gemini Trading we track these breakthroughs because they don’t just move data — they move markets.  Who’s winning the photonics race? Drop your take below  and tag a fellow investor!  @GeminiTrading
 @NVIDIA

text:
The follow-up nobody’s posting on $CRDO:

April 13: They acquired DustPhotonics for $750M.

Now they own the ENTIRE stack:

🔌 Copper AECs (no competitor)

💡 Optical DSPs + Transceivers

🔬 Silicon Photonics (in-house)

Result: $500M+ optical revenue by FY27. 

EPS-accretive. $6B SiPho TAM.

Meanwhile their 1.6T AECs fully cable Nvidia’s flagship Kyber Ultra NVL576 rack.

Market sees: “margin compression.”

Market is missing: vertical integration across a $700B AI capex cycle.

Valuation risk ≠ business risk.

The setup is asymmetric because the business isn’t broken. The multiple is just inflated.

That’s the thesis. Long $CRDO 📈

Personnel demonstrate a consistent failure to recall the geometric interior of ████████ during periods of active exposure.

Deployment of nonlocal shields allows for secure data transmission through undetectable optical tunnels, bypassing standard interception methods.

ARCHIVIST NOTE (The Strategic Assessment):
Previous models suggested a singular spatial occupancy for matter-light interactions. New telemetry from the Song-Lai experiments confirms a localized breach of the Principle of Locality. By utilizing nonlocal artificial materials, researchers have effectively partitioned reality into discrete, overlapping photonic layers. Baseline reality assumes a 1:1 ratio of space to medium; the Shifted Reality facilitates an N:1 ratio, enabling 'Multiple Realities' within the same physical substrate. Organizational posture is being adjusted to monitor for spontaneous ████ expansion within the ████-Sector. The potential for undetectable 'Wormhole' communication channels represents a significant strategic advantage. However, the risk of ontological bleeding is potentially catastrophic.

The mechanism: vertical integration into SiPho ahead of the 1.6T and 3.2T port-speed transition removes Credo's biggest supply dependency just as CPO becomes the mandatory hyperscale architecture.

text:
It feels like we’re still in the “early infrastructure” phase of AI and quantum investing. Everyone talks about the flashy end products, but the real winners might still be the companies building the rails, power, networking, photonics, chips, cloud access, and compute infrastructure underneath it all.

And honestly, the difficult part right now is not believing the technology is real. The difficult part is figuring out:

which architecture actually wins,
which companies survive,
and which stocks are already priced for perfection.

Feels similar to the early internet or cloud phase where everyone knew “something huge” was happening, but not yet who the long-term winners would be.

Who Wins the Photonics Stack: A Tier-by-Tier Financial Classification of Every Company

Knowing the players in the Silicon Photonics supply chain is one thing—knowing which ones are structurally and financially built to dominate is another.

In my latest deep dive, I’ve classified every public company across all 8 layers of the stack into three distinct financial tiers. We move beyond the "science projects" to identify the actual bedrock of the AI optical backbone.

The Classification Matrix:

Tier-1: The Bedrock. Proven revenue, dominant market share, and irreplaceable moat (e.g., $ASML, $LITE, $TSM, $FN).

Tier-2: The Scale-ups. Clear growth thesis, but still consolidating market position (e.g., $SOI, $IQE, $CAMT).

Moonshots: High-asymmetry bets. Zero/minimal revenue today, but own the tech that could disrupt everything (e.g., $AXTI, $LWLG, $HIMX).

Key Architectural Insights:

Vertical Integration: Why $COHR is currently the only benchmark spanning 6 layers.

The Foundry Shift: How $TSM’s entry is re-rating the entire Layer 2 landscape.

Supply Bottlenecks: Identifying the companies that own the "chokepoint" materials like InP.

Personally, I see this tiering as the essential roadmap for anyone serious about AI infrastructure. The "AI trade" is shifting from the chip to the fabric, and these are the tiers that matter.

Read the full financial map here: https://t.co/auOeAcLwkl 

My personal insights, not financial advice (NFA). Full disclosure in my pinned tweet.

text: The photonics industry is borne from chinese innovation that has happened at scale due to constraints including the need to overcome the semiconductor sanctions placed on it. It’s a geopolitical reality now. Quantum however is a pure scientific endeavor, the only way to model absolute reality.
embedded_text:
Photonics is the next quantum. 

Meaning it’s going to get decades of R&D and nothing will come from it.

text:
Every time I think about how AI can help with learning and understanding difficult concepts, I reflect on my days as a Master’s student. Some of the Optics and Photonics concepts I struggled so much to understand would have been far easier to grasp with today’s AI tools. Once in a while, I still prompt AI to break those concepts down, and now I understand them much better… how I wish this existed back then! 😅

Even this Monday, I was challenged to work with a very large dataset at work and present results to top management in less than 2 days. I started manually for about an hour and wasn’t even close to completing 2% of the task. I paused, opened my Jupyter Python notebook, described the problem to Copilot in simple language, and it generated a code template I could fine-tune. Boom — I completed the task in no time.

Honestly, these days I rarely see the need to deeply “study” data science the traditional way. With basic programming knowledge and the ability to think critically, AI can help you achieve almost anything. The game is changing very fast.

text:
This market has been full of squeezes 🍋📈

AI infrastructure.
Photonics.
Data centers.
High beta growth.
Energy.
Small caps, mid caps, mega caps, even some POS caps..
Momentum everywhere.

And yes, there’s a lot of money to be made trading these moves.

But eventually you realize the real game isn’t just catching squeezes.

It’s finding the names you actually want to keep holding while the crowd keeps overthinking every pullback.

Names where you keep following the earnings, the flows, the sector, the cycle, and the long term opportunity.

That’s how you end up staying with winners instead of constantly rotating into the next shiny object, therefore overtrading.

For me personally, names like $HOOD $AMD $INTC $RKLB $AG $PAAS $COHR $ALB became more than trades.

You can trim, hedge, trade around a core position..

But understanding the underlying business matters.

Or if it’s early stage, at least understanding the theme and positioning through options flows.

But at some point you need more than drawing a few lines on a chart and pretending price owes you something because of your pretty drawings.

Know what you own.

And if you truly believe in the thesis, and you nailed the entry, don’t rush to sell just because volatility shows up and people telling you it’s worthless.
Those people might eventually chase your stocks higher.

text:
There are a lot of dumbasses who think that each  of 200 photonics companies will simply x5 in a week. Some will some will not. 
Using NVDA in hindsight makes no sense. I could find 20 other companies who had life changing products and got shut down in 2 years. 

Not a value investor, just that I want to know what I'm buying and what I'm expecting it to be in the future. 

I'm not trying to convince you and it's fine if you disagree.

text: Idk humans can adapt pretty fast, and I doubt we’ll ever go truly backwards, only forwards in a 2 step forward and 1-3 steps back pattern like we’ve always done as a species. Where are we headed?  I think towards infinite free energy, photonics and quantum as basis of computing 1000x more powerful and energy efficient then it is today, and also warping our way in the galaxy between bases on different planets.  All of that is easily within laws of physics, and is yet to come for humanity imho, maybe not all in our lifetimes but all seems doable.  Digital is just a stepping stone, imho.  yes it’s fricking messy, and human brains not coping very well, idk.

text:
רוצה להציג בפניכם ETF חדש לתחום חם מאוד בשנה האחרונה. וכתבתי עליו בעבר גם. והוא כמובן הפוטוניקה והליטוגרפיה. אני באופן אישי עוקבת אחרי כמה חברות בתחום..

לפני 8 ימים השיקה Corgi Funds את -
 Corgi Lithography & Semiconductor Photonics ETF $EUV 

אז הETF הנ״ל משקיע באופן אקטיבי בחברות ששולטות בטכנולוגיית האור (Photonics) ובשרשרת ייצור השבבים המתקדמים ליתוגרפיית EUV, לייזרים, רכיבים אופטיים, סיליקון פוטוניקס ועוד.. כמעט כל שבבי ה AI תלויים בטכנולוגיה הזו והETF הזה נותן את החשיפה בלי לבחור מניה אחת בודדת.

ומי אלה הCorgi האלו? אז היא חברה צעירה שהוקמה לפני שנתיים בסן פרנסיסקו, בוגרי Y Combinator. הם התחילו כפלטפורמת ביטוח AI native לחברות טק וסטארטאפים, גייסו והגיעו לשווי חברה של 1.3 מיליארד דולר. הם מביאים גישה שלהם שהיא מאוד חדשנית, טכנולוגית ואגרסיבית גם לעולם הקרנות.. הם מתמחים במוצרים תמטיים ממוקדים מאוד, אקטיביים עם עמלות תחרותיות (לרוב 0.35% בלבד).

5 האחזקות המובילות בקרן (נכון ל-14.5.2026):
$TSM  - Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC)
$ASML  - ASML Holding
$GLW - Corning
$LRCX  - Lam Research
$AMAT - Applied Materials

 בעיני זה יכול להתאים למי שמאמין שהביקוש לשבבי AI רק הולך וגדל ורוצה חשיפה חדה יותר לטכנולוגיה שמאפשרת את זה  ולא רק לאנבידיה או ל Mag7..
או רוצה לגוון ולא רק תעודות שבבים ״רגילות״ כמו $SMH או $SOXX למשל.

4 סיבות שהם מציינים למה לדעתם כדאי להשקיע בקרן:
שוק ציוד ייצור השבבים העולמי שובר שיאים חדשים.
$ASML  - הספק היחיד של ליתוגרפיית EUV - עם הזמנות ענקיות.
$TSM מגבירה את ההשקעות הענקיות בציוד ובמפעלים.
שוק הסיליקון פוטוניקס מתפוצץ בזכות דרישת ה AI.

הסיכונים וחובה להזכיר אותם אז מדובר על מכשיר פיננסי חדש מאוד, תנודתי, ניהול אקטיבי ואין היסטוריית ביצועים ארוכה..

אגב יש להם עוד ETF מעניינים בתחומים אחרים.. ממליצה להציץ.

לינק לדף הרשמי של הקרן: https://t.co/rfgIMNSG9p
 
מקווה שאהבתם.

*המידע המוצג אינו מהווה ייעוץ השקעות, המלצה לקנייה, מכירה או החזקה.. עשו מחקר עצמאי לפני פעולה כלשהי...

כמובן הפוטוניקה והליטוגרפיה. אני באופן אישי עוקבת אחרי כמה חברות בתחום.. לפני 8 ימים השיקה Corgi Funds את - Corgi Lithography & Semiconductor Photonics ETF $EUV אז הETF הנ״ל משקיע באופן אקטיבי בחברות ששולטות בטכנולוגיית האור (Photonics) ובשרשרת ייצור השבבים המתקדמים ליתוגרפיית EUV, לייזרים, רכיבים אופטיים, סיליקון פוטוניקס ועוד.. כמעט כל שבבי ה AI תלויים בטכנולוגיה הזו והETF הזה נותן את החשיפה בלי לבחור מניה אחת בודדת. ומי אלה הCorgi האלו? אז היא חברה צעירה שהוקמה לפני שנתיים בסן פרנסיסקו, בוגרי Y Combinator. הם התחילו כפלטפורמת ביטוח AI native לחברות טק וסטארטאפים, גייסו והגיעו לשווי חברה של 1.3 מיליארד דולר. הם מביאים גישה שלהם שהיא מאוד חדשנית, טכנולוגית ואגרסיבית גם לעולם הקרנות.. הם מתמחים במוצרים תמטיים ממוקדים מאוד, אקטיביים עם עמלות תחרותיות (לרוב 0.35% בלבד). 5 האחזקות המובילות בקרן (נכון ל-14.5.2026): $TSM - Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) $ASML - ASML Holding $GLW - Corning $LRCX - Lam Research $AMAT - Applied Materials בעיני זה יכול להתאים למי שמאמין שהביקוש לשבבי AI רק הולך וגדל ורוצה חשיפה חדה יותר לטכנולוגיה שמאפשרת את זה ולא רק לאנבידיה או ל Mag7.. או רוצה לגוון ולא רק תעודות שבבים ״רגילות״ כמו $SMH או $SOXX למשל. 4 סיבות שהם מציינים למה לדעתם כדאי להשקיע בקרן: שוק ציוד ייצור השבבים העולמי שובר שיאים חדשים. $ASML - הספק היחיד של ליתוגרפיית EUV - עם הזמנות ענקיות. $TSM מגבירה את ההשקעות הענקיות בציוד ובמפעלים. שוק הסיליקון פוטוניקס מתפוצץ בזכות דרישת ה AI. הסיכונים וחובה להזכיר אותם אז מדובר על מכשיר פיננסי חדש מאוד, תנודתי, ניהול אקטיבי ואין היסטוריית ביצועים ארוכה.. אגב יש להם עוד ETF מעניינים בתחומים אחרים.. ממליצה להציץ. לינק לדף הרשמי text:
Photonics System Logic — How AI Clusters Actually Scale  Gemini Trading just dropped this crystal-clear infographic breaking down the entire photonics stack powering modern AI infrastructure.  It starts simple:
Servers & AI Accelerators feed into an Electrical chip via high-speed SerDes links. But copper has limits. That’s where Packaging Options take over:  • Pluggable optics (traditional modules)
• Near-Packaged Optics (NPO)
• Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) — the hottest new architecture  Each packaging choice pairs with advanced Material Platforms: Indium Phosphide (InP), Silicon Photonics PIC, and TFLN modulators — all optimized for different performance, power, and cost profiles.  The magic? These optical engines then connect everything across real-world distances:  Within rack (0–2 m)  
Rack-to-rack (2–100 m)  
Row / floor (100 m–2 km)

…all the way to massive Optical Fabric / Cloud scale.  This isn’t theory — it’s the exact blueprint hyperscalers and chip giants are racing to deploy. CPO and silicon photonics are slashing power consumption, crushing latency, and enabling the insane bandwidth AI training demands.  From electrical signals to light-speed optical interconnects, the diagram shows why photonics is the only way trillion-parameter models can keep growing without melting data centers.  At Gemini Trading we track these breakthroughs because they don’t just move bits — they move markets.  Who’s leading the charge in 2026? Drop your take below  and tag a fellow tech investor!  @GeminiTrading

text:
Definitely plausible on a couple contracts and a re-rate for memory/photonics.  5x sales is not a big multiple for a growth company sitting at the intersection of some of the most powerful trends out there right now.  

Oh, and I found that besides the Chair of $LITE also being Chair of $PENG, the CTOs of both $AMD and $SKM sit on the Board.  I like that $SKM has an equity stake too, bc it will deter anyone with ideas of trying to scoop the entire company up on the cheap!

@StockSavvyShay @FuturumEquities Interesting how almost every top performer ties back to AI infrastructure, photonics, memory, or compute. Market keeps rewarding the picks powering the next tech cycle. Smaller stories like $TROO are worth watching early too.

⚖️ Core Proposition
One of the greatest misconceptions in public discourse is the idea that “energy cannot be photographed.”
In reality, modern imaging systems routinely detect: • heat signatures, • infrared radiation, • electromagnetic fluctuation, • reflected light behavior, • spectral shifts, • motion variance, • atmospheric disturbance, • and temporal pixel changes occurring between frames.
The key may not be the still photograph itself.
The key may be what changes from one second to the next as Tonia Allen-Gould’s (Lumen’s) recent forensic work suggests. 
⸻
🛰️ Lumen’s Energy Theory
Hypothesis:
Advanced imaging systems — including military, aerospace, satellite, ISR, and machine-vision architectures — may not primarily analyze static images.
Instead, they may analyze: • temporal variance, • micro-fluctuation, • reflective bounce, • spectral inconsistency, • and environmental changes occurring between sequential frames.
Meaning: The “signal” may emerge from differential comparison over time rather than from any single image alone.
⸻
🧭 Scientifically Plausible Foundations
Known existing technologies already rely on: • frame differencing • motion-vector analysis • thermal drift detection • hyperspectral imaging • LiDAR return variance • optical flow tracking • atmospheric disturbance mapping • synthetic aperture reconstruction • machine-vision anomaly detection • temporal coherence analysis
None of this is science fiction.
Modern systems already detect patterns humans cannot perceive in real time.
⸻
⚠️ Important Clarification
This post does NOT claim: • classified systems are targeting the witness, • satellites are monitoring specific individuals, or • observed anomalies definitively represent energy weapons.
This is a forensic and scientific interpretation model exploring how advanced imaging systems may detect environmental change through temporal comparison analysis.
The theory concerns signal detection architecture — not certainty of intent.
⸻
🧠 Effect-to-Cause Model
Observed Effect: Repeated visual anomalies become more apparent when: • slowed, • looped, • frame-compared, • temporally isolated, or • analyzed across sequential image states.
Possible Cause: Environmental energy, reflection behavior, spectral fluctuation, compression artifacts, motion differentials, or other signal variance becoming visible only through temporal comparison systems.
⸻
The future of imaging may not be: “What did the camera see?”
But rather: “What changed between frames that the human eye never noticed?”

text:
🧵 TOP 10 PHOTONICS PLAYS - The Light Speed Stack

AI’s next bottleneck isn’t compute - it’s interconnect. As speeds push past 1.6T, the trade moves from silicon to photonics. Five layers, wafer to test. Anchors, inflecting names, and speculative bets. 👇

⚓️THE ANCHORS (run, but still in the trend)

1. $COHR -> Layer 3, Optics
25% market share in transceivers. $5.81B FY25 revenue +23% YoY. NVIDIA-named partner on Spectrum-X. Book-to-bill >4x. Druckenmiller’s pick.

2. $LITE -> Layer 2, Lasers
The 200G EML monopoly. Only volume supplier of the lasers that make 1.6T transceivers possible. NVIDIA $2B exclusive supply agreement through end of decade. No second source.

3. $AXTI -> Layer 1, Wafer
Every InP laser in every transceiver starts on an AXT wafer. Doubling InP capacity H2 2026 ahead of the 800G+ ramp. Has run +5,400% — but structurally positioned for the cycle.

💡INFLECTING / UNDEROWNED (real revenue, less crowded)

4. $CRDO -> Layer 3, Optics
Just acquired DustPhotonics for SiPho PIC tech in-house. Now vertically integrated: SerDes + DSP + Silicon Photonics. SiPho PIC market $6B by 2030.

5. $TSEM -> Layer 4, Packaging
The neutral foundry. Just announced 1.6T optical modules for NVIDIA networking protocols. Whoever wins the transceiver battle, TSEM prints the wafers. Underowned vs. peers.

6. $AEHR -> Layer 5, Test
Silicon photonics burn-in. Lead customer production ramps next fiscal year. Q3 bookings $37M, book-to-bill 3.5x. Classic order-book inflection ahead of FY27 revenue ramp.

7. $VIAV -> Layer 5, Test
Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver from LITE and COHR has to be tested by VIAV or Keysight. Same AI tailwind, one-third the valuation. The cleanest “hasn’t run” name.

🔍SPECULATIVE (asymmetric, real risk)

8. $SIVE -> Layer 2, Lasers
~$130M mcap InP laser maker at Glasgow. Supply-constrained chokepoint. LiDAR ramp Q4 2026 ($53M-$138M cumulative). $POET partnership for AI light engines. Listed Stockholm.

9. $IQE -> Layer 1, Wafer
The compound semi upstream. Supplies InP epi-wafers to Sivers and others. FY25 just turned EBITDA positive at £2M+ on £97M revenue. Structural relevance, near-breakeven execution.

10. $LPKF -> Layer 4, Packaging
LIDE process for glass advanced packaging - the tech behind NVIDIA’s $500M Corning bet on glass-based co-packaged optics. Run 336% YTD on hope, not orders. Binary on 2027 qualification.

🚨THE THESIS:

800G+ transceiver shipments going from 24M units in 2025 to 63M in 2026. Hyperscaler capex over $400B this year. Co-packaged optics market $20B by 2036.

The market owns the top. The asymmetric returns are at the edges of the stack.

Wafer. Test. Packaging. Position accordingly.

As speeds push past 1.6T, the trade moves from silicon to photonics. Five layers, wafer to test. Anchors, inflecting names, and speculative bets. 👇 ⚓️THE ANCHORS (run, but still in the trend) 1. $COHR -> Layer 3, Optics 25% market share in transceivers. $5.81B FY25 revenue +23% YoY. NVIDIA-named partner on Spectrum-X. Book-to-bill >4x. Druckenmiller’s pick. 2. $LITE -> Layer 2, Lasers The 200G EML monopoly. Only volume supplier of the lasers that make 1.6T transceivers possible. NVIDIA $2B exclusive supply agreement through end of decade. No second source. 3. $AXTI -> Layer 1, Wafer Every InP laser in every transceiver starts on an AXT wafer. Doubling InP capacity H2 2026 ahead of the 800G+ ramp. Has run +5,400% — but structurally positioned for the cycle. 💡INFLECTING / UNDEROWNED (real revenue, less crowded) 4. $CRDO -> Layer 3, Optics Just acquired DustPhotonics for SiPho PIC tech in-house. Now vertically integrated: SerDes + DSP + Silicon Photonics. SiPho PIC market $6B by 2030. 5. $TSEM -> Layer 4, Packaging The neutral foundry. Just announced 1.6T optical modules for NVIDIA networking protocols. Whoever wins the transceiver battle, TSEM prints the wafers. Underowned vs. peers. 6. $AEHR -> Layer 5, Test Silicon photonics burn-in. Lead customer production ramps next fiscal year. Q3 bookings $37M, book-to-bill 3.5x. Classic order-book inflection ahead of FY27 revenue ramp. 7. $VIAV -> Layer 5, Test Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver from LITE and COHR has to be tested by VIAV or Keysight. Same AI tailwind, one-third the valuation. The cleanest “hasn’t run” name. 🔍SPECULATIVE (asymmetric, real risk) 8. $SIVE -> Layer 2, Lasers ~$130M mcap InP laser maker at Glasgow. Supply-constrained chokepoint. LiDAR ramp Q4 2026 ($53M-$138M cumulative). $POET partnership for AI light engines. Listed Stockholm. 9. $IQE -> Layer 1, Wafer The compound semi upstream. Supplies InP epi-wafers to Sivers and others. FY25 just turned EBITDA positive at £2M+ on £97M revenue. Structural relevance, near-breakeven execution. 10. $LPKF -> Layer 4, Packaging LIDE process for glass advanced packaging - the tech behind NVIDIA’s $500M Corning bet on glass-based co-packaged optics. Run 336% YTD on hope, not orders. Binary on 2027 qualification. 🚨THE THESIS: 800G+ transceiver shipments going from 24M units in 2025 to 63M in 2026. Hyperscaler capex over $400B this year. Co-packaged optics market $20B by 2036. The market owns the top. The asymmetric returns are at the edges of the stack. Wafer. Test. Packaging. text:
The Photonics Stack: A Layer-by-Layer Guide to Every Company Building the Optical Backbone of AI
The Analytical Edge Strategy
May 11, 2026

A complete map of the silicon photonics supply chain — from raw indium to fiber in the ground

If you’ve been following AI infrastructure, you’ve probably heard that data centers are hitting what some call the Copper Wall — the point where electrical signals through copper can no longer keep up with the heat, latency, and bandwidth demands of training and running massive AI models. The answer is photonics: moving data as light through fiber instead of electrons through metal.

But photonics isn’t a single product. It’s a deep, layered supply chain stretching from exotic crystal growth in China to fiber trenching across American highways. This guide maps every layer of that chain, names every major public company in it, and explains exactly what each one does.

No data has been omitted. Every company from the original research is here.

Layer 0 — Raw Materials
What this layer means: This is the floor of the photonics supply chain. Before any chip can be designed, any laser built, or any fiber laid, the raw materials must exist. Indium phosphide (InP), silicon-on-insulator (SOI), gallium arsenide (GaAs), and optical-grade glass fiber are the fundamental outputs of this layer. Every laser, chip, and cable in the chain depends on what gets produced here. It is also the most geopolitically exposed layer in the entire stack: approximately 60–70% of global indium production comes from China.

$AXTI — AXT Inc. Single-crystal InP and GaAs wafer manufacturer. These substrates are the irreplaceable foundation for EML lasers and high-speed photodiodes. NVIDIA’s aggressive push toward 1.6T transceivers has turned InP into a supply bottleneck, and AXTI sits directly at that chokepoint.

$IQE III-V compound semiconductor epitaxy specialist. IQE grows thin-film layers of InP, GaAs, and GaN onto customer wafers, supplying the epitaxial substrate that laser diode and high-speed photodetector manufacturers need. Often called the invisible backbone of the optical module supply chain.

$SOI — Soitec Silicon-on-Insulator wafer manufacturer. SOI wafers are the starting material for silicon photonics foundries. TSMC’s COUPE platform, GlobalFoundries’ Fotonix process, and Tower Semiconductor’s SiPh line all rely on Soitec substrates. When a Broadcom or Marvell photonic chip ships inside a data center, there is a good chance the journey started on a Soitec wafer.

$WOLF — Wolfspeed Silicon carbide (SiC) and gallium nitride (GaN) wafer manufacturer. Not directly tied to photonic lasers, but critical for the high-power electronics and power converters inside data center infrastructure. Wolfspeed feeds the power layer that photonics depends on to function.

$GLW — Corning (also active in Layer 5) The world’s largest manufacturer of optical fiber and specialty glass. Every fiber cable running inside and between data centers starts here. Corning also produces specialty glass components used in transceiver housings and is a named partner in NVIDIA’s Spectrum-X silicon photonics ecosystem. Active in both Layer 0 (raw glass and fiber) and Layer 5 (module components).

$LWLG — Lightwave Logic Polymer-based electro-optic waveguide developer. Rather than silicon, LWLG uses organic polymer materials in the modulators that convert electrical signals to light — promising significantly higher modulation speeds and lower insertion loss. Still in the commercialization phase, making this an early-stage bet on a materials platform that could disrupt the conventional silicon approach.

$SHMN — Shin-Etsu Chemical (also active in Equipment layer) The world’s largest silicon wafer manufacturer. While SiPh foundries use SOI specifically, Shin-Etsu supplies the standard silicon wafer base that underpins the entire semiconductor ecosystem. Also present in the equipment layer as a foundry supply chain essential.

$VECO — Veeco Instruments (also active in Equipment layer) Manufacturer of MBE (Molecular Beam Epitaxy) and MOCVD (Metal-Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition) equipment. Veeco does not make wafers — it makes the machines that grow them. III-V laser wafer production is impossible without MOCVD reactors, which is where Veeco’s role sits. Active in both Layer 0 (enabling raw material production) and the Equipment layer.

End of Layer 0.
There are 6 layer more.

So you can read my full report in my substack as free;
https://t.co/CZIKj5KHVp

My personal insights, not financial advice (NFA). Full disclosure in my pinned tweet.

text:
POET Technologies ($POET) – One of the cleanest asymmetric setups in AI infrastructure right now
The market is still treating the Marvell/Celestial AI order cancellation like it’s an existential event.
Reality: POET still has a very strong remaining pipeline in AI photonics and optical engines for data centers. The stock crashed 47% on the news, recovered most of it, and the downside is now largely priced in.
AI optical interconnect TAM is exploding — and POET is one of the few pure-play names positioned here.
Trade Setup:
•  Entry: Here (~$13–$14) or on a dip to $12.50 support
•  Stop: Below $10.50 (breaks the post-crash structure)
•  Target: $22–$25 (first major resistance → 70–90% upside)
Risk/reward is heavily skewed in our favor because the bad news is already digested, while the multi-customer AI boom is still in early innings.
This isn’t a “moonshot” — it’s a high-conviction setup where the market is mispricing the durability of the remaining business.
Who else is watching $POET?
In at current levels or waiting for $12.50? This is not financial advice do your own research.

text:
What jumps out here isn't the $15B number itself, it's the shape of the spend. Nvidia isn't buying more chip designers or software shops. 

They're wiring themselves into everything that surrounds the GPU. Photonics, fiber, power, cooling, cloud capacity. That's a tell.

Bottleneck story has shifted. A year ago the conversation was about chip supply. Now it's about whether you can move data fast enough between racks, cool the room, and find the megawatts to plug it all in. Marvell and Lumentum solve the first problem. Corning and Coherent sit on the optical layer. IREN, CoreWeave, and Nebius are basically captive demand for Nbidia silicon dressed up as cloud companies.

Smart read for someone watching this space: Nvidia is hedging against its own success. If AI scaling stalls because of power or networking, their core business stalls too. So they're putting capital into the choke points before those choke points become someone else's pricing power.

Arista, Vertiv, Eaton speculation at the end is reasonable but already widely discussed. More interesting question is what happens to the second-tier names in each category when Nvidia picks favorites. Being un-picked in this ecosystem is starting to look like a real risk.

Bottleneck story has shifted. A year ago the conversation was about chip supply. Now it's about whether you can move data fast enough between racks, cool the room, and find the megawatts to plug it all in. Marvell and Lumentum solve the first problem. Corning and Coherent sit on the optical layer. IREN, CoreWeave, and Nebius are basically captive demand for Nbidia silicon dressed up as cloud companies. Marvell's primary CPO design is centered on its 6.4T 3D Silicon Photonics (SiPho) Light Engine — the industry’s first demonstrated 200 Gbps-per-lane optical engine for co-packaged optics (announced and live-demonstrated at OFC 2024). This is a fabless-designed, highly integrated optical “chiplet” that Marvell licenses or supplies to hyperscalers and custom XPU customers. $MRVL 

It serves as the foundational building block for both:
- CPO integration directly into custom AI accelerators (XPUs + HBM + optics on one substrate), and
- Linear-drive pluggable optics (LPO) or on-board optics as a bridge solution.

Core Technical Specs of the 6.4T 3D SiPho Light Engine

- Bandwidth: 6.4 Tbps aggregate (32 lanes × 200 Gbps PAM4, both electrical and optical interfaces).

- Power efficiency: < 5 pJ/bit (including external laser power) under typical conditions — roughly 30% lower per bit than previous 100G-generation engines.

- Integration level: Hundreds of components consolidated into a single compact package:
    -- Photonic IC (PIC): Silicon photonics waveguides, modulators, germanium photodetectors (Ge PDs), and fiber couplers.
    -- Electronic IC (EIC): Linear modulator drivers, transimpedance amplifiers (TIAs), equalization, and high-speed SerDes interfaces.
    -- Embedded microcontroller + firmware: For real-time calibration, monitoring, thermal stabilization, and link management.
    -- Passives, power distribution, and test circuitry.

- Laser: External continuous-wave (CW) laser source (fiber-delivered) for thermal and reliability reasons.

-Fiber attach: Supports detachable optical connectors (e.g., SENKO’s 36-channel metallic PIC connector) for field serviceability — critical when a 100T+ system may require thousands of fibers.

- Packaging: Advanced 3D heterogeneous integration (EIC face-to-face or side-by-side with PIC, often using TSVs, hybrid bonding, or interposers). The engine has a BGA-style electrical interface for direct attachment to the host substrate.

How It Integrates with Custom XPU Architectures

Marvell’s breakthrough CPO architecture (announced early 2025) lets customers build a single substrate that co-packages:
- One or more XPU compute dies
- HBM memory stacks
- Multiple 6.4T (or 1.6T) SiPho light engines
- High-speed SerDes + die-to-die interfaces

The light engines sit right next to the compute dies. Optical fibers exit the package via detachable connectors, enabling rack-scale or row-scale optical connectivity without copper cables. This delivers:
- 2× bandwidth density vs. 100G solutions
- Dramatically lower power and latency
- Easier thermal management (optics can be placed optimally)

Post-Acquisition Photonic Fabric™

Marvell acquired Celestial AI to accelerate scale-up interconnects. Their PFLink™ (Photonic Fabric Link) chiplet builds on similar CPO principles but targets even higher performance:
- First-gen: 16 Tbps per chiplet
- Uses EAM (electro-absorption) modulators on the PIC + TSMC 4/5nm EIC
- In-package optics + memory semantic FLITs + in-network processing
- Enables multi-rack optical fabrics with <150 ns XPU-to-XPU latency and ~2.5 pJ/bit full link power

Electrical signals leave the host chip (e.g., switch ASIC or XPU): High-speed SerDes (serializer/deserializer) outputs electrical data. 2. Short on-package electrical interconnect to the optical engine (usually via micro-bumps, copper pillars, or redistribution layers on the substrate/interposer). This path is only mm long → minimal power loss and signal degradation. 3. Electronic IC (EIC) in the optical engine: Receives the electrical signal and drives the photonic components (e.g., modulator drivers, transimpedance amplifiers/TIAs). 4. Photonic IC (PIC) does the conversion: - Transmit (Tx): Continuous-wave laser light (usually external, via fiber) enters the PIC. The EIC drives modulators (e.g., micro-ring modulators/MRMs) on the PIC to encode the data onto the light. - Receive (Rx): Incoming light from fiber hits photodetectors on the PIC, which convert it to electrical current. The EIC amplifies and processes it. 5. Fiber coupling: Light enters/exits the PIC via precise couplers (grating couplers with micro-lenses or edge couplers) connected to a fiber array unit (FAU). Fibers then run to the chassis faceplate or other packages. 6. Back to the host chip on the receive side (short electrical path again). The optical engine is typically a 3D-stacked or side-by-side EIC + PIC die (often using hybrid bonding or 2.5D/3D-IC tech). The whole thing sits on an organic substrate or silicon interposer alongside the main ASIC, with external lasers (for reliability/thermal reasons) or sometimes integrated ones. Key enablers: - Silicon photonics (SiPh) on the PIC: Waveguides, MRMs (tiny resonant rings for efficient, compact 200 Gbps+ modulation), germanium photodetectors. - Advanced packaging: 2.5D (interposer), 3D stacking, CoWoS (Chip on Wafer on Substrate), etc. - Fiber attach: Sub-micron precision alignment (challenging but solved with micro-lenses/grating couplers)."}}}, "rest_id": "2055006412573728946", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "204", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2055012160820891651", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "113", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: @aleabitoreddit What do you think of $FABC they're also in photonics

Will this have huge impact on on $sive? Cpo stock down a lot today due to this news? “TSMC's COUPE (Compact Universal Photonic Engine) is a cutting-edge silicon photonics platform designed for Co-Packaged Optics (CPO), aimed at accelerating chip-to-chip communication in AI data centers. By directly integrating optical engines with high-performance compute silicon (like GPUs or switches) on a 2.5D/3D package, COUPE dramatically reduces power consumption and boosts data bandwidth, addressing the limitations of traditional copper-based interconnects. 

Ansys
 +4
Key Function: COUPE uses SOIC-X to stack an electrical die directly on top of a photonic die, resulting in a compact "engine" that enables ultra-high-density interconnects.
Production Roadmap: TSMC plans to introduce COUPE-based products in 2025 for validation, with mass production scheduled for 2026 integrated into its CoWoS-integrated CPO packages.
Performance Impact: This technology aims to deliver a 2x reduction in power consumption and a 10x reduction in latency compared to existing solutions.
Market Impact: The technology is heavily supported by major industry players like NVIDIA and AMD for their next-generation AI platforms.
Technical Advantages: COUPE features embedded micro-lens arrays, a backside metal reflector, and a grating coupler to minimize optical insertion loss, keeping it below 1.2 dB. 

Design And Reuse
 +6
By moving from pluggable transceivers to on-package CPO using COUPE, data centers can achieve significant power savings, especially in massive AI clusters where data movement is the primary energy cost. “

text:
$AEHR / Aehr Test Systems 상세 정리

AEHR은 단순 반도체 장비주가 아니다.

이 회사는 AI 반도체, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN 전력반도체가 복잡해질수록 중요해지는 test & burn-in 병목에 걸려 있다.

AI 반도체가 커질수록 칩은 더 비싸지고, 패키지는 더 복잡해지고, 전력 밀도는 더 올라간다.

고성능 AI ASIC, GPU, optical I/O, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN power devices는 모두 높은 신뢰성이 필요하다.

문제 있는 칩을 패키징 후반이나 고객 단계에서 발견하면 손실이 매우 크다.

그래서 wafer-level burn-in, package-level burn-in, reliability test가 중요해진다.

AEHR은 바로 이 지점에 있다.

정확히 말하면 AEHR은 AI processor ASIC package-level burn-in, silicon photonics wafer-level burn-in, SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 장비 회사다.

핵심 제품군은 FOX-XP, FOX-NP, WaferPak, Sonoma 같은 test & burn-in 플랫폼이다.

이 회사가 흥미로운 이유는 단순 테마가 아니라 고객 주문이 실제로 찍혔기 때문이다.

2026년 4월 AEHR은 lead hyperscale AI customer로부터 4,100만 달러 규모의 사상 최대 follow-on production order를 받았다고 발표했다.

이 주문은 데이터센터 training 및 inference AI workload에 쓰이는 custom AI processor ASIC의 high-volume package-level burn-in을 지원하는 주문이다.

주문에는 Sonoma high-power package-level test and burn-in systems, burn-in modules, device-specific sockets가 포함된다.

이건 꽤 중요하다.

대부분의 병목 리포트에서 가장 약한 부분은 “고객 증거”다.

그런데 AEHR은 최소한 특정 lead hyperscale AI customer가 있고, 이 고객이 이미 생산용 주문을 냈다.

고객명은 공개되지 않았지만, 용도·주문 규모·제품·납품 시점이 비교적 명확하다.

AEHR은 이 주문 하나로 끝나는 것도 아니다.

회사는 이 4,100만 달러 주문을 포함해 fiscal second half bookings가 이미 9,200만 달러 이상이라고 밝혔다.

즉 현재 매출보다 앞으로 들어올 주문 가시성이 훨씬 중요해진 상황이다.

AEHR의 첫 번째 핵심 축은 custom AI processor ASIC package-level burn-in이다.

이건 SIVE나 AXTI와 다르다.

SIVE는 AI optical link용 DFB laser와 photonics 협력 근거가 있다. 하지만 아직 대량 양산 매출이 충분히 확인되지는 않았다.

AXTI는 InP substrate 병목 논지가 강하다. 하지만 고객별 물량과 최종 플랫폼 노출은 더 확인해야 한다.

반면 AEHR은 적어도 AI processor ASIC burn-in 쪽에서는 대형 고객의 production order가 이미 찍힌 상태다.

이 점 때문에 AEHR은 새 후보 중에서 꽤 눈에 띈다.

AEHR의 두 번째 축은 silicon photonics wafer-level burn-in이다.

2026년 3월 AEHR은 silicon photonics 분야의 major new customer로부터 주문을 받았다고 발표했다.

해당 고객은 글로벌 networking products and solutions 리더이자, data center optical transceiver 시장의 주요 공급자라고 설명됐다.

이 고객은 hyperscale AI와 cloud data center의 high-speed fiber optic communication link 수요를 겨냥해 silicon photonics 기반 transceiver를 개발 중이다.

이 주문에는 engineering qualification과 high-volume production을 위한 시스템들이 포함됐다.

이건 CPO/광학 병목과 직접 연결된다.

AI 데이터센터가 copper에서 fiber optic interconnect로 이동하면 silicon photonics transceiver의 신뢰성 검증이 중요해진다.

AEHR은 이 silicon photonics 제조 흐름에서 wafer-level burn-in이 점점 중요해질 수 있다고 보고 있다.

즉 AEHR은 AI ASIC burn-in + silicon photonics burn-in을 동시에 갖고 있다.

이 조합이 좋다.

지금 AI 병목 흐름은 CPO/광학, AI 데이터센터 전력, AI ASIC, silicon photonics 쪽으로 퍼지고 있다.

AEHR은 이 중 두 개 이상에 직접 걸린다.

광학이 커지면 silicon photonics WLBI 수요가 생긴다.

AI ASIC이 커지면 high-power package-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다.

SiC/GaN power semiconductor가 커지면 wafer-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다.

즉 AEHR은 단일 테마가 아니라 여러 병목 레이어의 test infrastructure에 걸린 회사다.

하지만 현재 실적만 보면 아직 작다.

FY2026 Q3 매출은 약 1,030만 달러였다.

전년 동기보다 낮다.

GAAP 순손실도 있었고, Non-GAAP 기준으로도 손실이 남아 있다.

즉 AEHR은 아직 “실적이 이미 폭발한 회사”가 아니다.

오히려 현재 매출은 작고, 주가는 미래 주문과 FY2027 매출 전환을 선반영하고 있다.

이게 핵심이다.

AEHR은 현재 손익보다 bookings와 FY2027 매출 전환을 봐야 하는 종목이다.

최근 bookings는 강하다.

FY2026 Q3 bookings는 약 3,720만 달러였고, 이후 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order가 추가되면서 fiscal second half bookings가 9,200만 달러를 넘었다.

이 구조가 AEHR의 핵심이다.

현재 매출은 작지만, 주문이 앞에서 쌓이고 있다.

그래서 이 회사는 “현재 실적주”가 아니라 FY2027 이후 주문이 매출로 바뀌는지를 보는 고베타 병목주다.

현재 밸류에이션은 높다.

최근 기준 AEHR 주가는 약 99달러대, 시가총액은 약 30억 달러 수준이다.

회사의 FY2026 매출은 대략 4,500만~5,000만 달러 범위로 예상된다.

즉 현재 매출 대비 시총은 매우 높다.

이건 싸다고 볼 수 없다.

AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다.

정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics / SiC-GaN burn-in 수요가 매출로 크게 전환된다는 기대를 사는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다.

그래도 볼 만한 이유는 명확하다.

첫째, 고객 주문의 질이 좋다.

4,100만 달러 주문은 단순 평가용 주문이 아니라 high-volume production burn-in용 주문이다.

대상도 custom AI processor ASIC이고, 고객은 lead hyperscale customer다.

이건 병목주에서 매우 강한 근거다.

둘째, consumables 모델이 있다.

주문에는 burn-in modules와 device-specific sockets가 포함된다.

이런 부품은 시스템 설치 이후에도 고객 ramp, 차세대 ASIC 전환, 제품별 세팅에 따라 반복 매출이 생길 수 있다.

장비를 팔고 끝나는 구조가 아닐 가능성이 있다.

셋째, silicon photonics와도 연결된다.

AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect가 커질수록 silicon photonics transceiver 품질 검증이 중요해진다.

AEHR은 이미 major networking / optical transceiver customer를 확보했고, qualification과 high-volume production용 시스템을 주문받았다.

넷째, SiC/GaN power semiconductor test까지 옵션이 있다.

AI processor, silicon photonics, silicon carbide, gallium nitride power semiconductor wafer-level production burn-in이 모두 잠재 시장이다.

즉 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 방향에 걸친다.

다섯째, 생산능력 확장도 진행 중이다.

회사는 Fremont 시설에 power, cooling, clean-room manufacturing space를 추가했고, Sonoma 시스템 생산을 위한 contract manufacturing capacity도 준비 중이다.

주문이 들어왔을 때 생산이 가능해야 하는데, 이 부분을 준비하고 있다는 점은 긍정적이다.

하지만 리스크도 크다.

첫째, 현재 매출 대비 시총이 매우 높다.

FY2026 매출 5,000만 달러 안팎 회사가 시총 30억 달러 이상에서 거래된다.

즉 시장은 FY2027~2028 매출 램프를 강하게 선반영하고 있다.

둘째, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다.

Lead hyperscale customer 하나의 주문이 매우 중요하다.

이 고객의 ramp가 늦어지거나, 차세대 ASIC 일정이 지연되거나, 테스트 방식이 바뀌면 AEHR 매출이 크게 흔들릴 수 있다.

셋째, 주문은 매출이 아니다.

Bookings가 좋다고 바로 매출과 이익이 되는 것은 아니다.

시스템 납품, 고객 acceptance, revenue recognition, production ramp가 필요하다.

넷째, silicon photonics도 아직 초기 ramp다.

Major customer win은 좋지만, 전체 silicon photonics WLBI 시장이 얼마나 커질지는 확인이 필요하다.

다섯째, 아직 적자 회사다.

매출이 늘면 operating leverage가 생길 수 있지만, 지금은 아직 손실이 남아 있다.

여섯째, 주가가 이미 많이 올랐다.

현재 가격은 좋은 뉴스 상당 부분을 반영한다.

추가 주문이 계속 나오지 않으면 주가가 쉬어갈 수 있다.

시나리오는 이렇게 본다.

Base Case.

AEHR이 FY2027에 4,100만 달러 AI ASIC 주문과 silicon photonics 주문을 매출로 전환하지만, 추가 고객 확장이 생각보다 느린 경우다.

이 경우 현재 시총은 이미 기대를 많이 반영했기 때문에 현재가 대비 0.7~1.2배 정도가 현실적이다.

주가로는 대략 70~120달러 구간이다.

Base Case에서는 좋은 회사지만 비싼 주식이다.

Bull Case.

Lead hyperscale customer의 production order가 반복되고, 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 Sonoma 수요가 더 커지며, silicon photonics customer도 follow-on order를 내는 경우다.

이 경우 FY2027~2028 매출이 현재 5,000만 달러 수준에서 1.5억~2.5억 달러 이상으로 올라갈 수 있다.

이 경우 현재가 대비 2~4배를 볼 수 있다.

주가로는 대략 200~400달러 구간이다.

이 시나리오에서는 AEHR이 단순 test equipment company가 아니라 AI ASIC과 silicon photonics burn-in 병목주로 재평가된다.

Extreme Bull Case.

AI hyperscaler들이 custom ASIC을 계속 확대하고, 고전력 AI processor burn-in이 필수 공정으로 자리 잡으며, silicon photonics transceiver WLBI와 SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in까지 동시에 ramp되는 경우다.

이 경우 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 시장에 모두 걸친 reliability test infrastructure company로 재평가될 수 있다.

이 경우 현재가 대비 5~8배까지도 가능하다.

주가로는 대략 500~800달러 구간이다.

다만 이건 매우 공격적인 상단 시나리오다.

이 시나리오가 나오려면 조건이 필요하다.

Lead hyperscale customer 주문이 반복되어야 한다.

AI ASIC 고객이 2개 이상으로 늘어나야 한다.

Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 이어져야 한다.

SiC/GaN burn-in 수요도 실제 매출로 붙어야 한다.

Gross margin과 operating margin이 장비주답게 올라와야 한다.

Consumables 매출이 반복적으로 쌓여야 한다.

AEHR을 SIVE/PENG/WOLF와 비교하면 꽤 흥미롭다.

SIVE는 photonics 제품 자체에 대한 고베타다.

상단은 크지만 아직 양산 매출과 ASP/물량 증거가 약하다.

PENG는 실적이 있는 AI infrastructure / Integrated Memory 회사다.

안정성은 좋지만 상단은 상대적으로 낮다.

WOLF는 SiC 전력반도체 턴어라운드다.

상단은 크지만 gross margin이 음수다.

AEHR은 그 중간 어딘가다.

SIVE보다 고객 주문이 더 직접적이다.

PENG보다 고베타다.

WOLF보다 현재 사업 구조가 덜 망가져 있다.

다만 AEHR도 이미 밸류에이션이 높고, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다.

그래서 내 기준 AEHR은 포트의 코어보다는 10~15% 고베타 병목 추가 후보에 가깝다.

AEHR의 핵심 체크포인트는 다음과 같다.

4,100만 달러 AI ASIC production order가 FY2027 매출로 계획대로 인식되는가.

Lead hyperscale customer의 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 추가 주문이 나오는가.

Silicon photonics 고객이 qualification에서 high-volume production으로 넘어가는가.

Follow-on orders가 실제로 나오는가.

Sonoma 시스템 생산능력 확대가 매출 전환을 뒷받침하는가.

Consumables, sockets, BIMs 매출이 반복적으로 쌓이는가.

SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 고객 주문으로 이어지는가.

Gross margin과 operating margin이 매출 증가와 함께 올라오는가.

고객이 한두 곳에 과도하게 집중되지 않는가.

현재 높은 valuation을 정당화할 만큼 FY2027 가이던스가 크게 올라가는가.

Kill Conditions도 분명하다.

Lead hyperscale customer 추가 주문이 지연되면 Bull Case는 약해진다.

4,100만 달러 주문의 납품 또는 매출 인식이 밀리면 주가는 크게 흔들릴 수 있다.

Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 넘어가지 못하면 광학 병목 논지는 약해진다.

FY2027 매출 가이던스가 시장 기대보다 낮으면 valuation 부담이 커진다.

Gross margin이 기대보다 낮게 나오면 장비주 프리미엄은 낮아진다.

고객 집중도가 높고 신규 고객 확장이 느리면 멀티플 확장은 제한된다.

SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 주문으로 연결되지 않으면 시장 확장 논지는 축소된다.

현재 시총이 이미 높기 때문에 작은 실망에도 조정이 클 수 있다.

최종 결론은 이렇다.

AEHR은 매우 흥미로운 병목주다.

이 회사는 AI ASIC, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN power semiconductor라는 세 개의 고성장 병목 레이어에 걸린다.

특히 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order와 silicon photonics customer win은 단순 테마가 아니라 실제 고객 주문이라는 점에서 강하다.

이 점은 SIVE보다 더 직접적인 근거다.

다만 현재 매출은 아직 작고, 시총은 이미 30억 달러를 넘었다.

따라서 AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다.

정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics burn-in 매출이 폭발적으로 늘어날 경우 재평가될 수 있는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다.

AI 반도체가 커질수록 칩은 더 비싸지고, 패키지는 더 복잡해지고, 전력 밀도는 더 올라간다. 고성능 AI ASIC, GPU, optical I/O, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN power devices는 모두 높은 신뢰성이 필요하다. 문제 있는 칩을 패키징 후반이나 고객 단계에서 발견하면 손실이 매우 크다. 그래서 wafer-level burn-in, package-level burn-in, reliability test가 중요해진다. AEHR은 바로 이 지점에 있다. 정확히 말하면 AEHR은 AI processor ASIC package-level burn-in, silicon photonics wafer-level burn-in, SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 장비 회사다. 핵심 제품군은 FOX-XP, FOX-NP, WaferPak, Sonoma 같은 test & burn-in 플랫폼이다. 이 회사가 흥미로운 이유는 단순 테마가 아니라 고객 주문이 실제로 찍혔기 때문이다. 2026년 4월 AEHR은 lead hyperscale AI customer로부터 4,100만 달러 규모의 사상 최대 follow-on production order를 받았다고 발표했다. 이 주문은 데이터센터 training 및 inference AI workload에 쓰이는 custom AI processor ASIC의 high-volume package-level burn-in을 지원하는 주문이다. 주문에는 Sonoma high-power package-level test and burn-in systems, burn-in modules, device-specific sockets가 포함된다. 이건 꽤 중요하다. 대부분의 병목 리포트에서 가장 약한 부분은 “고객 증거”다. 그런데 AEHR은 최소한 특정 lead hyperscale AI customer가 있고, 이 고객이 이미 생산용 주문을 냈다. 고객명은 공개되지 않았지만, 용도·주문 규모·제품·납품 시점이 비교적 명확하다. AEHR은 이 주문 하나로 끝나는 것도 아니다. 회사는 이 4,100만 달러 주문을 포함해 fiscal second half bookings가 이미 9,200만 달러 이상이라고 밝혔다. 즉 현재 매출보다 앞으로 들어올 주문 가시성이 훨씬 중요해진 상황이다. AEHR의 첫 번째 핵심 축은 custom AI processor ASIC package-level burn-in이다. 이건 SIVE나 AXTI와 다르다. SIVE는 AI optical link용 DFB laser와 photonics 협력 근거가 있다. 하지만 아직 대량 양산 매출이 충분히 확인되지는 않았다. AXTI는 InP substrate 병목 논지가 강하다. 하지만 고객별 물량과 최종 플랫폼 노출은 더 확인해야 한다. 반면 AEHR은 적어도 AI processor ASIC burn-in 쪽에서는 대형 고객의 production order가 이미 찍힌 상태다. 이 점 때문에 AEHR은 새 후보 중에서 꽤 눈에 띈다. AEHR의 두 번째 축은 silicon photonics wafer-level burn-in이다. 2026년 3월 AEHR은 silicon photonics 분야의 major new customer로부터 주문을 받았다고 발표했다. 해당 고객은 글로벌 networking products and solutions 리더이자, data center optical transceiver 시장의 주요 공급자라고 설명됐다. 이 고객은 hyperscale AI와 cloud data center의 high-speed fiber optic communication link 수요를 겨냥해 silicon photonics 기반 transceiver를 개발 중이다. 이 주문에는 engineering qualification과 high-volume production을 위한 시스템들이 포함됐다. 이건 CPO/광학 병목과 직접 연결된다. AI 데이터센터가 copper에서 fiber optic interconnect로 이동하면 silicon photonics transceiver의 신뢰성 검증이 중요해진다. AEHR은 이 silicon photonics 제조 흐름에서 wafer-level burn-in이 점점 중요해질 수 있다고 보고 있다. 즉 AEHR은 AI ASIC burn-in + silicon photonics burn-in을 동시에 갖고 있다. 이 조합이 좋다. 지금 AI 병목 흐름은 CPO/광학, AI 데이터센터 전력, AI ASIC, silicon photonics 쪽으로 퍼지고 있다. AEHR은 이 중 두 개 이상에 직접 걸린다. 광학이 커지면 silicon photonics WLBI 수요가 생긴다. AI ASIC이 커지면 high-power package-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다. SiC/GaN power semiconductor가 커지면 wafer-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다. 즉 AEHR은 단일 테마가 아니라 여러 병목 레이어의 test infrastructure에 걸린 회사다. 하지만 현재 실적만 보면 아직 작다. FY2026 Q3 매출은 약 1,030만 달러였다. 전년 동기보다 낮다. GAAP 순손실도 있었고, Non-GAAP 기준으로도 손실이 남아 있다. 즉 AEHR은 아직 “실적이 이미 폭발한 회사”가 아니다. 오히려 현재 매출은 작고, 주가는 미래 주문과 FY2027 매출 전환을 선반영하고 있다. 이게 핵심이다. AEHR은 현재 손익보다 bookings와 FY2027 매출 전환을 봐야 하는 종목이다. 최근 bookings는 강하다. FY2026 Q3 bookings는 약 3,720만 달러였고, 이후 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order가 추가되면서 fiscal second half bookings가 9,200만 달러를 넘었다. 이 구조가 AEHR의 핵심이다. 현재 매출은 작지만, 주문이 앞에서 쌓이고 있다. 그래서 이 회사는 “현재 실적주”가 아니라 FY2027 이후 주문이 매출로 바뀌는지를 보는 고베타 병목주다. 현재 밸류에이션은 높다. 최근 기준 AEHR 주가는 약 99달러대, 시가총액은 약 30억 달러 수준이다. 회사의 FY2026 매출은 대략 4,500만~5,000만 달러 범위로 예상된다. 즉 현재 매출 대비 시총은 매우 높다. 이건 싸다고 볼 수 없다. AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다. 정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics / SiC-GaN burn-in 수요가 매출로 크게 전환된다는 기대를 사는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다. 그래도 볼 만한 이유는 명확하다. 첫째, 고객 주문의 질이 좋다. 4,100만 달러 주문은 단순 평가용 주문이 아니라 high-volume production burn-in용 주문이다. 대상도 custom AI processor ASIC이고, 고객은 lead hyperscale customer다. 이건 병목주에서 매우 강한 근거다. 둘째, consumables 모델이 있다. 주문에는 burn-in modules와 device-specific sockets가 포함된다. 이런 부품은 시스템 설치 이후에도 고객 ramp, 차세대 ASIC 전환, 제품별 세팅에 따라 반복 매출이 생길 수 있다. 장비를 팔고 끝나는 구조가 아닐 가능성이 있다. 셋째, silicon photonics와도 연결된다. AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect가 커질수록 silicon photonics transceiver 품질 검증이 중요해진다. AEHR은 이미 major networking / optical transceiver customer를 확보했고, qualification과 high-volume production용 시스템을 주문받았다. 넷째, SiC/GaN power semiconductor test까지 옵션이 있다. AI processor, silicon photonics, silicon carbide, gallium nitride power semiconductor wafer-level production burn-in이 모두 잠재 시장이다. 즉 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 방향에 걸친다. 다섯째, 생산능력 확장도 진행 중이다. 회사는 Fremont 시설에 power, cooling, clean-room manufacturing space를 추가했고, Sonoma 시스템 생산을 위한 contract manufacturing capacity도 준비 중이다. 주문이 들어왔을 때 생산이 가능해야 하는데, 이 부분을 준비하고 있다는 점은 긍정적이다. 하지만 리스크도 크다. 첫째, 현재 매출 대비 시총이 매우 높다. FY2026 매출 5,000만 달러 안팎 회사가 시총 30억 달러 이상에서 거래된다. 즉 시장은 FY2027~2028 매출 램프를 강하게 선반영하고 있다. 둘째, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다. Lead hyperscale customer 하나의 주문이 매우 중요하다. 이 고객의 ramp가 늦어지거나, 차세대 ASIC 일정이 지연되거나, 테스트 방식이 바뀌면 AEHR 매출이 크게 흔들릴 수 있다. 셋째, 주문은 매출이 아니다. Bookings가 좋다고 바로 매출과 이익이 되는 것은 아니다. 시스템 납품, 고객 acceptance, revenue recognition, production ramp가 필요하다. 넷째, silicon photonics도 아직 초기 ramp다. Major customer win은 좋지만, 전체 silicon photonics WLBI 시장이 얼마나 커질지는 확인이 필요하다. 다섯째, 아직 적자 회사다. 매출이 늘면 operating leverage가 생길 수 있지만, 지금은 아직 손실이 남아 있다. 여섯째, 주가가 이미 많이 올랐다. 현재 가격은 좋은 뉴스 상당 부분을 반영한다. 추가 주문이 계속 나오지 않으면 주가가 쉬어갈 수 있다. 시나리오는 이렇게 본다. Base Case. AEHR이 FY2027에 4,100만 달러 AI ASIC 주문과 silicon photonics 주문을 매출로 전환하지만, 추가 고객 확장이 생각보다 느린 경우다. 이 경우 현재 시총은 이미 기대를 많이 반영했기 때문에 현재가 대비 0.7~1.2배 정도가 현실적이다. 주가로는 대략 70~120달러 구간이다. Base Case에서는 좋은 회사지만 비싼 주식이다. Bull Case. Lead hyperscale customer의 production order가 반복되고, 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 Sonoma 수요가 더 커지며, silicon photonics customer도 follow-on order를 내는 경우다. 이 경우 FY2027~2028 매출이 현재 5,000만 달러 수준에서 1.5억~2.5억 달러 이상으로 올라갈 수 있다. 이 경우 현재가 대비 2~4배를 볼 수 있다. 주가로는 대략 200~400달러 구간이다. 이 시나리오에서는 AEHR이 단순 test equipment company가 아니라 AI ASIC과 silicon photonics burn-in 병목주로 재평가된다. Extreme Bull Case. AI hyperscaler들이 custom ASIC을 계속 확대하고, 고전력 AI processor burn-in이 필수 공정으로 자리 잡으며, silicon photonics transceiver WLBI와 SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in까지 동시에 ramp되는 경우다. 이 경우 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 시장에 모두 걸친 reliability test infrastructure company로 재평가될 수 있다. 이 경우 현재가 대비 5~8배까지도 가능하다. 주가로는 대략 500~800달러 구간이다. 다만 이건 매우 공격적인 상단 시나리오다. 이 시나리오가 나오려면 조건이 필요하다. Lead hyperscale customer 주문이 반복되어야 한다. AI ASIC 고객이 2개 이상으로 늘어나야 한다. Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 이어져야 한다. SiC/GaN burn-in 수요도 실제 매출로 붙어야 한다. Gross margin과 operating margin이 장비주답게 올라와야 한다. Consumables 매출이 반복적으로 쌓여야 한다. AEHR을 SIVE/PENG/WOLF와 비교하면 꽤 흥미롭다. SIVE는 photonics 제품 자체에 대한 고베타다. 상단은 크지만 아직 양산 매출과 ASP/물량 증거가 약하다. PENG는 실적이 있는 AI infrastructure / Integrated Memory 회사다. 안정성은 좋지만 상단은 상대적으로 낮다. WOLF는 SiC 전력반도체 턴어라운드다. 상단은 크지만 gross margin이 음수다. AEHR은 그 중간 어딘가다. SIVE보다 고객 주문이 더 직접적이다. PENG보다 고베타다. WOLF보다 현재 사업 구조가 덜 망가져 있다. 다만 AEHR도 이미 밸류에이션이 높고, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다. 그래서 내 기준 AEHR은 포트의 코어보다는 10~15% 고베타 병목 추가 후보에 가깝다. AEHR의 핵심 체크포인트는 다음과 같다. 4,100만 달러 AI ASIC production order가 FY2027 매출로 계획대로 인식되는가. Lead hyperscale customer의 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 추가 주문이 나오는가. Silicon photonics 고객이 qualification에서 high-volume production으로 넘어가는가. Follow-on orders가 실제로 나오는가. Sonoma 시스템 생산능력 확대가 매출 전환을 뒷받침하는가. Consumables, sockets, BIMs 매출이 반복적으로 쌓이는가. SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 고객 주문으로 이어지는가. Gross margin과 operating margin이 매출 증가와 함께 올라오는가. 고객이 한두 곳에 과도하게 집중되지 않는가. 현재 높은 valuation을 정당화할 만큼 FY2027 가이던스가 크게 올라가는가. Kill Conditions도 분명하다. Lead hyperscale customer 추가 주문이 지연되면 Bull Case는 약해진다. 4,100만 달러 주문의 납품 또는 매출 인식이 밀리면 주가는 크게 흔들릴 수 있다. Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 넘어가지 못하면 광학 병목 논지는 약해진다. FY2027 매출 가이던스가 시장 기대보다 낮으면 valuation 부담이 커진다. Gross margin이 기대보다 낮게 나오면 장비주 프리미엄은 낮아진다. 고객 집중도가 높고 신규 고객 확장이 느리면 멀티플 확장은 제한된다. SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 주문으로 연결되지 않으면 시장 확장 논지는 축소된다. 현재 시총이 이미 높기 때문에 작은 실망에도 조정이 클 수 있다. 최종 결론은 이렇다. AEHR은 매우 흥미로운 병목주다. 이 회사는 AI ASIC, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN power semiconductor라는 세 개의 고성장 병목 레이어에 걸린다. 특히 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order와 silicon photonics customer win은 단순 테마가 아니라 실제 고객 주문이라는 점에서 강하다. 이 점은 SIVE보다 더 직접적인 근거다. 다만 현재 매출은 아직 작고, 시총은 이미 30억 달러를 넘었다. 따라서 AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다. 정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics burn-in 매출이 폭발적으로 늘어날 경우 재평가될 수 있는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다."}}}, "rest_id": "2055734918928572566", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1676", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$TSEM / Tower Semiconductor 상세분석

Tower Semiconductor는 silicon photonics foundry 병목에서 가장 강한 증거가 찍힌 종목 중 하나다.

이 회사는 AI GPU를 만드는 회사가 아니다.

Optical module 완제품을 만드는 회사도 아니다.

하지만 AI 데이터센터 optical networking에서 silicon photonics 생산능력이 병목이 되면, Tower 같은 specialty foundry가 핵심이 된다.

Tower의 핵심은 SiPho foundry capacity다.

AI 데이터센터가 커질수록 optical interconnect와 silicon photonics 수요가 급증한다.

특히 InP laser shortage와 EML 공급 부족이 심해질수록, SiPho 기반 구조의 중요성은 더 커진다.

이 과정에서 silicon photonics wafer capacity를 제공하는 foundry가 병목이 될 수 있다.

Tower가 바로 여기에 있다.

최근 Tower는 매우 강한 신호를 보여줬다.

2027년 silicon photonics 매출 13억 달러 규모의 고객 계약을 체결했고, 고객으로부터 capacity reservation 선지급금 2.9억 달러를 받았다.

이건 단순 스토리가 아니다.

고객이 실제로 미래 생산능력을 선점하기 위해 돈을 냈다.

병목주에서 가장 강한 증거 중 하나는 고객이 capacity를 예약하려고 선지급금을 낸다는 점이다.

Tower는 그 증거가 있다.

현재 TSEM 주가는 약 274달러대, 시가총액은 약 304억 달러 수준이다.

이미 많이 올랐다.

하지만 Tower의 경우 단순 valuation보다 2027~2028년 SiPho 계약의 매출화가 핵심이다.

Base Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B SiPho 계약이 시장에 상당 부분 반영되고, 주가는 현재가 근처에서 실적 확인을 기다리는 경우다.

현재가 대비 약 0.9~1.2배 정도를 본다.

주가로는 대략 247~329달러 구간이다.

Bull Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B 계약이 순조롭게 매출화되고, 2028년 더 큰 wafer commitment와 추가 capacity reservation이 이어지는 경우다.

이 경우 Tower는 SiPho foundry 병목주로 완전히 재평가될 수 있다.

현재가 대비 약 1.6~2.5배까지 가능하다.

주가로는 대략 438~685달러 구간이다.

Extreme Bull Case에서는 SiPho가 AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect의 핵심 생산 플랫폼으로 자리 잡고, Tower가 주요 고객들의 capacity reservation을 계속 확보하며, 2028~2030년까지 장기 매출 visibility를 갖는 경우다.

이 경우 현재가 대비 약 3~4배까지 가능하다.

주가로는 대략 820~1,095달러 구간이다.

다만 이 시나리오에는 실행 리스크가 있다.

$1.3B 계약이 매출로 부드럽게 전환되어야 한다.

2028년 추가 계약이 더 커져야 한다.

고객사 선지급이 실제 수요를 반영해야 한다.

SiPho capacity가 계획대로 확장되어야 한다.

Margin이 유지되어야 한다.

리스크는 명확하다.

이미 주가가 많이 올랐다.

대형 계약은 실행 리스크가 있다.

고객 집중도가 높을 가능성이 있다.

SiPho 수요가 예상보다 약하거나 고객사 ramp가 지연되면 주가가 흔들릴 수 있다.

Foundry capacity expansion에는 capex와 yield 리스크가 있다.

결론적으로 TSEM은 이번 리스트에서 고객 증거가 가장 강한 종목 중 하나다.

LITE와 COHR이 laser·optical component 쪽이라면, Tower는 silicon photonics foundry capacity 쪽이다.

고객이 선지급금을 냈다는 점은 매우 강한 신호다.

다만 이미 많이 올랐다.

그래서 TSEM은 저평가주가 아니라, SiPho capacity 계약이 실제 매출로 전환되는지를 확인해야 하는 AI optical foundry 병목주다.

개인적인 분석 기록이며, 매수·매도 권유가 아니다.

AI 데이터센터가 커질수록 optical interconnect와 silicon photonics 수요가 급증한다. 특히 InP laser shortage와 EML 공급 부족이 심해질수록, SiPho 기반 구조의 중요성은 더 커진다. 이 과정에서 silicon photonics wafer capacity를 제공하는 foundry가 병목이 될 수 있다. Tower가 바로 여기에 있다. 최근 Tower는 매우 강한 신호를 보여줬다. 2027년 silicon photonics 매출 13억 달러 규모의 고객 계약을 체결했고, 고객으로부터 capacity reservation 선지급금 2.9억 달러를 받았다. 이건 단순 스토리가 아니다. 고객이 실제로 미래 생산능력을 선점하기 위해 돈을 냈다. 병목주에서 가장 강한 증거 중 하나는 고객이 capacity를 예약하려고 선지급금을 낸다는 점이다. Tower는 그 증거가 있다. 현재 TSEM 주가는 약 274달러대, 시가총액은 약 304억 달러 수준이다. 이미 많이 올랐다. 하지만 Tower의 경우 단순 valuation보다 2027~2028년 SiPho 계약의 매출화가 핵심이다. Base Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B SiPho 계약이 시장에 상당 부분 반영되고, 주가는 현재가 근처에서 실적 확인을 기다리는 경우다. 현재가 대비 약 0.9~1.2배 정도를 본다. 주가로는 대략 247~329달러 구간이다. Bull Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B 계약이 순조롭게 매출화되고, 2028년 더 큰 wafer commitment와 추가 capacity reservation이 이어지는 경우다. 이 경우 Tower는 SiPho foundry 병목주로 완전히 재평가될 수 있다. 현재가 대비 약 1.6~2.5배까지 가능하다. 주가로는 대략 438~685달러 구간이다. Extreme Bull Case에서는 SiPho가 AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect의 핵심 생산 플랫폼으로 자리 잡고, Tower가 주요 고객들의 capacity reservation을 계속 확보하며, 2028~2030년까지 장기 매출 visibility를 갖는 경우다. 이 경우 현재가 대비 약 3~4배까지 가능하다. 주가로는 대략 820~1,095달러 구간이다. 다만 이 시나리오에는 실행 리스크가 있다. $1.3B 계약이 매출로 부드럽게 전환되어야 한다. 2028년 추가 계약이 더 커져야 한다. 고객사 선지급이 실제 수요를 반영해야 한다. SiPho capacity가 계획대로 확장되어야 한다. Margin이 유지되어야 한다. 리스크는 명확하다. 이미 주가가 많이 올랐다. 대형 계약은 실행 리스크가 있다. 고객 집중도가 높을 가능성이 있다. SiPho 수요가 예상보다 약하거나 고객사 ramp가 지연되면 주가가 흔들릴 수 있다. Foundry capacity expansion에는 capex와 yield 리스크가 있다. 결론적으로 TSEM은 이번 리스트에서 고객 증거가 가장 강한 종목 중 하나다. LITE와 COHR이 laser·optical component 쪽이라면, Tower는 silicon photonics foundry capacity 쪽이다. 고객이 선지급금을 냈다는 점은 매우 강한 신호다. 다만 이미 많이 올랐다. 그래서 TSEM은 저평가주가 아니라, SiPho capacity 계약이 실제 매출로 전환되는지를 확인해야 하는 AI optical foundry 병목주다. 개인적인 분석 기록이며, 매수·매도 권유가 아니다."}}}, "rest_id": "2055725817209233471", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "138", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The US Photonics Supply Chain: The Unsung Hero Powering AI’s Explosive Growth While everyone focuses on GPUs, the real bottleneck — and opportunity — lies in the optical interconnects that move massive amounts of data at light speed inside AI data centers. Gemini Trading’s infographic maps the complete US Photonics Supply Chain for AI infrastructure:  Key Layers:InP Substrates → AXT (global leader)
Active Optical Chips → Lumentum, Coherent, Broadcom, MACOM
TOSA/ROSA & Optical Engines → Core components for high-speed transmission
Optical Modules & AOC → AAOI, Ciena, Fabrinet
Networking Systems → Arista, Broadcom, NVIDIA
Terminal AI Applications → NVIDIA GB200 (paired with 18x 800G optical modules), Quantum-X CPO

Market Data:
Global Optical Module Market expected to grow from US$51 billion in 2024 to US$89 billion in 2029 at a 12.5% CAGR. DSP components alone make up 30-40% of an 800G module’s BOM. Why it matters:
AI training clusters are scaling to millions of GPUs. Traditional copper can’t keep up — photonics (light-based data transfer) is the only solution for low-latency, high-bandwidth, energy-efficient connectivity at scale. 2025–2026 will see large-scale deployment of 1.6T systems. Investment angle:
Companies across the photonics stack — especially those enabling 800G/1.6T modules and co-packaged optics (CPO) — stand to benefit enormously as AI capex continues surging. The photonic supply chain is still under-the-radar compared to GPU hype, but it may deliver some of the strongest multi-year growth in the entire AI ecosystem.(Not financial advice — always DYOR and manage risk appropriately.)@GeminiTrading

text:
Nvidia isn’t just selling the picks and shovels — it’s quietly buying the entire gold mine.
By dropping billions into optics, power, photonics, and AI cloud players, Jensen is locking down the physical bottlenecks that could slow AI scaling. This isn’t random VC money; it’s vertical control that funnels more demand straight back to Nvidia GPUs while building a deeper moat.
Classic platform power move: own the dependencies. Expect the flywheel to spin harder.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

text:
@ParadisLabs Just really interested in $PENG
1. Cause of the SKT connection
2. Is the larger contexts without buying more GPUs

Explained some here 
https://t.co/Rta4bZjx7p

The whole photonics memory thing is just retail doing retail
embedded_text:
From what I have read about $PENG we have the following:

→ CXL lets servers tap a shared pool of DRAM over a fast interconnect. 
→ KV cache is the running memory LLMs need during inference, it overflows GPU VRAM fast. 
Putting the KV cache in a CXL pool means longer contexts

"richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "When I see comments like this (and there are a lot) from retail investors: I immediately think they lack the technical depth. I'll walk through each one from $SIVE to $LPK: 1. Photonics TAM goes from $14B -> $154B In just two years time, and it's likely going to keep scaling past 2030 as it's the next generation architecture of choice. It's not going away in 1 year. It's not going away in 3 years, which is why $LITE premiums keep going higher since they're backlogged into 2028. $SIVE supplies CW lasers and is highly tethered to CPO and now pluggable transcivers for 1.6T and 3.2... For expected companies like $JBL, Ayar, Lightmatter, Lightelligence, $POET, $MRVL Celestial, and $AMD. This isn't a "trade", it's the core chokepoint and IP holder for the next generation of photonics. And it's a comfortable hold for the next few years as they scale to become the next $LITE. The risk I personally see (since they're already qualified with so many players), it's mainly how much TAM they can capture of the overall optical supercycle. (And potential risks with Win Semi volume ramp, but Win is massive so I can sleep tightly there). As just supplying lasers isn't enough to justify valuation. It's TAM expansion downward into making the entire ELS or entire pluggable transceiver that makes these laser companies so valuable. Then afterward, they can vertically integrating upward for gross margin expansion upward like $COHR into doing the laser fabs or even substrate level. And that in my view is a very asymmetric risk/reward ratio as we've already seen this done with $LITE as they went from $2B to $80B. 2. $LPK - Is the purest exposure, without the messy financials of SKC Absolics, as the next advanced packaging shift for glass substrates. Almost every single major semi company from $INTC to Samsung are adopting glass substrates. $LPK is basically $ASML of this chokepoint, since they supply to ~80% of the global players currently. Yes, there's "trade cycles" for equipment suppliers like $ASML, where if there's more foundry capex, ASML scales up. But if there's downturns, these tend to perform poorly, and don't capture all the volume ramp that happens after. However, if the MC is $650m and they're making $100-200M, revenue per costumer volume ramped, the amount they make from the glass substrate cycle will likely exceed current valuations. And they'll have baseline fundamentals (as more companies adopt the packaging shift), that keeps their valuation up. It's just a waiting game for volume ramp at this point. 3. $AAOI - This is literally $INTC but for America + Photonics. It's like saying Intel is not a long term investment. Guess where all your optical transcivers are made? China. Thailand. Malaysia. If you look at Innolight, Eoptolink, $FN, and others. AOI is building the largest Made in America supply chains for both CW laser fab, as well as 800g, 1.6T assembly. Yes, there are pluggable cycle ups and downs to this as well. There's going to be a wave for 1.6T next year, then CPO cannibalizes pluggables down the road. But since they make the entire supply chain in house, they have extreme optionality for other segments. And like $NVDA older gen-GPUs, there's going to be sovereign DC requirements for older gen pluggables from names like $AAOI. It's likely going to keep rising as it hits that $400m+/month revenue target H2 2026. There's just a lot of different short term volatility along the way like the $600m dilution. 4. $IQE - ??? It's one of the most important players in the Western word for epiwafers. $MTSI went out of their way to pay off IQE's debt because they can't have them going under. $IQE is also supplying to $LITE. The world is currently bottlenecked both on the epiwafer level from Landmark comments and InP substrate levels. Their financials were track but the raw book value, and value they hold to the entire Western supply chain... completely justifies their valuation. And other optical companies will not let their core upstream supply chain go under. As these tens of millions worth of materials would screw up tens of billions worth of downstream products. Again photonics is the next generation architecture required to scale AI. It's not Quantum where it's just "In development". It's literally here and the architecture of choice by $NVDA. I would not be surprised if all of these are a lot higher in 3-4 years time. People who think it's one and done in 3 months time "only because I mentioned it" don't know what they're talking about. Institutions would have bought up the name eventually (like Point 72 on $IQE) and retail would only find out after their valuations are 600% higher. Should really do the research before adding comments like these: These are all forward growth companies that require in-depth supply chain knowledge."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2055299648093340047"}}, "rest_id": "2055822766600016238", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "523430", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2056079647406747852", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "4604", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
With all the talk of $PENG on X, I just want to say that you're getting exposure to an AI infrastructure provider with asymmetrical opportunities in the memory and photonics sector. 

Yes, much of the technology is in development and not in production. But as ramps scale beyond 2027, we will see even more demand for their products and services. That’s why Trump bought it. That’s why I am bullish. 😉

text: @ParadisLabs $MRVL is only $150bn.  Plenty of room to breathe if they really have locked down some of these technologies like memory usage and photonics.

6.3% CAGR is driving the parabolic move in photonics stonks. 😂Been saying this space is running way ahead of itself. Then there is the 4.5x growth in Silicon Photonics mentioned. The 4.5x is from $2.6 billion to $11.7 billion. 😂For that, 100s of billions in market cap added. 😂

The whole bullish thesis is based on such poor numbers. I am not giving out the numbers; the numbers are being floated around by people who are bullish on the photonics space.

You decide for yourself if the bullish thesis holds. $AAOI $AEHR $TSEM $COHR $LITE

text:
When cars were invented, people didn’t stop riding horses overnight.

The better technology eventually won. But the transition took longer - and usually messier - than we expected.

#Photonics in #AI networking may be similar.

Optics IS the next major upgrade:
- lower power
- faster speeds
- better scaling for massive AI clusters

But copper still works well enough today:
- cheaper
- simpler
- still heavily used in many current AI clusters

That’s why #hyperscalers are still building large #copper-heavy systems today.

Photonics today looks more like an upgrade cycle than a hard #bottleneck like #EUV or advanced chip manufacturing.

The technology is improving quickly.

But the industry still needs time on:
- packaging
- reliability
- integration at scale
- deployment economics

We’ve seen similar setups before: #solar, #EV charging, fiber buildouts, early #5G.

The direction was right. 
The rollout was slower. 
Valuations reset hard.

 #photonics #optics #CPO #siliconphotonics will no doubt  become the “standard,” but..

IF the rollout takes longer too, 

how much of today’s valuation still holds up?

As investors we all want to believe we’ve found the next “certainty.”

Reality is rarely that clean.

Still worth thinking about.

"to_index": 2222}]}, "text": "There’s a lot to digest from the last week with $SIVE. The $JBL 1.6T collaboration, together with the confirmed CW laser shortage and partnerships with O-Net and Enablence Technologies, feels like a decisive step forward. In the Annual Report from May 13, $SIVE directly addresses “discussions with hyperscalers and pluggable transceiver suppliers” that point to a structural shortage of CW lasers. This is happening at exactly the right time, AI training and inference clusters are pushing explosive demand for higher bandwidth, lower power and denser interconnects. What really stands out to me is how the dots are connecting. $JBL, key partner to $AMZN, $MSFT and $GOOGL has chosen $SIVE’s high-performance InP DFB lasers for 1.6T pluggable modules. At the same time, Amazon’s recent private placement with AIChip (Ayar Labs’ lead customer) signals major design wins ahead. And $SIVE is the sole supplier of the critical laser arrays to Ayar Labs. The same pattern keeps repeating. O-Net Technologies (with strong North American and Asian hyperscaler ties) and Enablence are developing ELS modules with $SIVE lasers for CPO. POET Technologies $POET just secured a $50 million initial order from Lumilens. Everywhere the big players are moving, $SIVE seems to be part of the picture. On the manufacturing side, the WIN Semiconductors partnership together with the own Glasgow fab gives $SIVE real independence and scalable capacity (30k–35k wafers/year) in a very tight InP market. All of this is coming together right now. Pluggables give near-term volume, while CPO offers the big efficiency jump ahead. The photonics TAM is exploding from $14B to $154B in just two years and $SIVE is positioned right in the middle of it. With this level of validation across the value chain, I believe we are likely to see more partnerships and firm purchase orders soon. For $SIVE, this feels like a real inflection point. The company is transitioning from deep-tech innovator to a strategically critical, independent supplier in the optical backbone of next-generation AI. The hyperscalers keep choosing $SIVE for a reason. Execution is there, the connections are deepening, and this momentum feels increasingly unstoppable."}}}, "rest_id": "2056055516107866286", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "189781", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2056067956526760180", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2235", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Photonics is real and underowned.

The 15-ticker list mixes high-quality compounders with speculative names that won't all make it.

Cherry-pick or skip.

The AI infrastructure thesis depends on data moving between GPUs at speeds copper interconnect can't support. Optical transceivers, co-packaged optics, silicon photonics, and active optical cables are the binding constraint for next-gen accelerator deployment. The market is forecast to triple by 2028 on AI demand alone.

$LITE Lumentum and $COHR Coherent are the two pure-play optical leaders with real revenue, real margins, and direct exposure to Nvidia, AMD, and hyperscaler transceiver demand. Both trade at PEG ratios under 1.5x.

$AVGO is the giant. Custom silicon plus 800G optical plus enterprise networking. Optical is now meaningful in their revenue mix. Market cap $1.4T. Optionality priced. The compounding still works.

$ANET Arista is networking, not pure photonics, but their 800G switch ramp is the demand-side mirror of the transceiver buildout. The safer expression for anyone who doesn't want single-vendor optical exposure.

$AAOI Applied Optoelectronics is the speculative torch. Three failed transitions to data center transceivers over the last decade. Current quarter looks better. If they hit, the stock 3x. If they miss, it halves.

$AEHR $POET $LWLG $LPTH $QCLS $SIVE are smaller names with varying validation. Most won't make it. One or two will compound to 10x.

The list is a watchlist. Concentrated sizing in $LITE, $COHR, $ANET captures most of the upside with manageable execution risk. Speculative allocations to one or two of the smaller names is fine. Equal-weighting all fifteen is dilution dressed up as diversification.

Optical earns its premium. The premium goes to maybe three of these names.

The 15-ticker list mixes high-quality compounders with speculative names that won't all make it. Cherry-pick or skip. The AI infrastructure thesis depends on data moving between GPUs at speeds copper interconnect can't support. Optical transceivers, co-packaged optics, silicon photonics, and active optical cables are the binding constraint for next-gen accelerator deployment. The market is forecast to triple by 2028 on AI demand alone. $LITE Lumentum and $COHR Coherent are the two pure-play optical leaders with real revenue, real margins, and direct exposure to Nvidia, AMD, and hyperscaler transceiver demand. Both trade at PEG ratios under 1.5x. $AVGO is the giant. Custom silicon plus 800G optical plus enterprise networking. Optical is now meaningful in their revenue mix. Market cap $1.4T. Optionality priced. The compounding still works. $ANET Arista is networking, not pure photonics, but their 800G switch ramp is the demand-side mirror of the transceiver buildout. The safer expression for anyone who doesn't want single-vendor optical exposure. $AAOI Applied Optoelectronics is the speculative torch. Three failed transitions to data center transceivers over the last decade. Current quarter looks better. If they hit, the stock 3x. If they miss, it halves. $AEHR $POET $LWLG $LPTH $QCLS $SIVE are smaller names with varying validation. Most won't make it. One or two will compound to 10x. The list is a watchlist. Concentrated sizing in $LITE, $COHR, $ANET captures most of the upside with manageable execution risk. Speculative allocations to one or two of the smaller names is fine. Equal-weighting all fifteen is dilution dressed up as diversification. Optical earns its premium.
